questions.q: Who directed the 2001 Hindi period drama film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' was written and directed by Ashutosh Gowariker.

questions.q: In which year was the film 'Lagaan' released?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' was released in 2001.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role of Bhuvan and also produced the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Aamir Khan played Bhuvan and produced 'Lagaan' under his Aamir Khan Productions banner.

questions.q: Around which sport does the plot of the film 'Lagaan' revolve?
questions.explanation: The story of 'Lagaan' centres on a cricket match between Indian villagers and British officers.

questions.q: The word 'Lagaan' in the film's title refers to which of the following?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' means tax or land revenue, the levy the villagers wager against in a cricket match.

questions.q: Which composer scored the music for the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman composed the music and background score for 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: The film 'Lagaan' was India's official entry in which category at the Academy Awards?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' was India's official submission for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar.

questions.q: In which historical period is the film 'Lagaan' set?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' is set in 1893, during the period of British colonial rule in India.

questions.q: What is the name of the village at the centre of the story in the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: The villagers of Champaner challenge the British to the cricket match in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Which actress played the female lead Gauri in the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Gracy Singh played Gauri, the village woman in love with Bhuvan, in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: What is the full title of the 2001 film commonly known as 'Lagaan'?
options: Lagaan: Once Upon a Time in India
questions.explanation: The film's complete title is 'Lagaan: Once Upon a Time in India'.

questions.q: Which British actress played Elizabeth Russell, the officer's sister who helps the villagers in 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Rachel Shelley played Elizabeth Russell, who teaches the villagers cricket in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Which actor played the antagonist Captain Andrew Russell in the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Paul Blackthorne played the arrogant Captain Andrew Russell, who imposes the wager in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Who wrote the lyrics for the songs of the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Javed Akhtar penned the lyrics for the songs of 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Which of these songs features in the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: 'Ghanan Ghanan', a song about awaited monsoon clouds, is from the 'Lagaan' soundtrack.

questions.q: At which annual ceremony did the film 'Lagaan' win the National Film Award for Best Popular Film providing wholesome entertainment?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' won at the 49th National Film Awards, held for films of 2001.

questions.q: In the film 'Lagaan', for how many years' tax does the wager promise relief if the villagers win the cricket match?
questions.explanation: Captain Russell wagers that the villagers will pay no 'lagaan' for three years if they win.

questions.q: Which legendary Bollywood figure narrated the opening voice-over of the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan provided the English-language narration that opens 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: At which international film festival did the film 'Lagaan' win an audience award in 2001?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' won the Audience Award (Prix du Public) at the Locarno International Film Festival in 2001.

questions.q: Which character in the film 'Lagaan' is an untouchable who becomes a key spin bowler for the village team?
questions.explanation: Kachra, the disabled untouchable, becomes the team's spin bowler in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: In the film 'Lagaan', a mute character communicates only through a drum he carries. What is his name?
questions.explanation: Bagha, the mute villager who plays the dhol, is one of the eleven players in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Which film won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi for the year that 'Lagaan' was released?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi at the 49th National Film Awards.

questions.q: In which year is the story of the film 'Lagaan' specifically set?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' is set in the year 1893 in the fictional Champaner region of colonial India.

questions.q: At the 74th Academy Awards, which film won the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar that 'Lagaan' was nominated for?
questions.explanation: The Bosnian film 'No Man's Land' won the Oscar over 'Lagaan' at the 74th Academy Awards.

questions.q: Before 'Lagaan', which two Indian films had been nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' was the third Indian film nominated, after 'Mother India' (1957) and 'Salaam Bombay!' (1988).

questions.q: In which Indian state was much of the film 'Lagaan' shot, including the village set built near Bhuj?
questions.explanation: The Champaner village set for 'Lagaan' was built near Bhuj in the Kutch region of Gujarat.

questions.q: Who served as the director of photography (cinematographer) on the film 'Lagaan'?
questions.explanation: Anil Mehta was the cinematographer on 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: Which playback singer voiced the devotional song 'O Paalanhaare' in the film 'Lagaan' alongside Udit Narayan and Sukhwinder Singh?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar lent her voice to 'O Paalanhaare' in 'Lagaan'.

questions.q: The film 'Lagaan' was a feature-length debut as producer for Aamir Khan under which production banner?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' was the first film produced under Aamir Khan Productions.

questions.q: Approximately how long is the runtime of the film 'Lagaan', making it one of the longer mainstream Hindi films?
questions.explanation: 'Lagaan' has a runtime of roughly 3 hours and 40 minutes.
